notes: Synthetic Part 2A text covering all 18 Items with a Table of Contents that repeats every Item title. Exercises the TOC dedupe heuristic.

inputs.text:
SYNTHETIC ADVISORY LLC
Form ADV Part 2A Brochure
March 2026

Item 1: Cover Page
This brochure provides information about the qualifications and business practices of Synthetic Advisory LLC.

Item 2: Material Changes
No material changes since the last annual amendment.

Item 3: Table of Contents
Item 1 Cover Page
Item 2 Material Changes
Item 3 Table of Contents
Item 4 Advisory Business
Item 5 Fees and Compensation
Item 6 Performance-Based Fees
Item 7 Types of Clients
Item 8 Methods of Analysis
Item 9 Disciplinary Information
Item 10 Other Financial Industry Activities and Affiliations
Item 11 Code of Ethics
Item 12 Brokerage Practices
Item 13 Review of Accounts
Item 14 Client Referrals and Other Compensation
Item 15 Custody
Item 16 Investment Discretion
Item 17 Voting Client Securities
Item 18 Financial Information

Item 4. Advisory Business
Synthetic Advisory LLC is a registered investment adviser founded in 2010, with approximately $1.2 billion in discretionary assets under management.

Item 5. Fees and Compensation
Advisory fees are charged as an annual percentage of assets under management, tiered as follows: 1.00% on the first $1 million, 0.85% on the next $4 million, 0.65% above $5 million.

Item 6. Performance-Based Fees and Side-By-Side Management
Not applicable. The firm does not accept performance-based fees.

Item 7. Types of Clients
The firm provides investment advice to high-net-worth individuals, trusts, estates, and charitable organizations.

Item 8. Methods of Analysis, Investment Strategies and Risk of Loss
The firm employs fundamental and quantitative analysis across global equity and fixed-income markets. Investing in securities involves risk of loss that clients should be prepared to bear.

Item 9. Disciplinary Information
The firm has no disciplinary history to disclose.

Item 10. Other Financial Industry Activities and Affiliations
The firm is not affiliated with a broker-dealer. One supervised person is a licensed attorney.

Item 11. Code of Ethics, Participation or Interest in Client Transactions and Personal Trading
The firm has adopted a Code of Ethics requiring quarterly personal-trading reports from all access persons.

Item 12. Brokerage Practices
The firm uses Charles Schwab and Fidelity as primary custodians. Soft-dollar arrangements are disclosed in the Form ADV Part 1.

Item 13. Review of Accounts
Client accounts are reviewed at least quarterly by a supervising portfolio manager.

Item 14. Client Referrals and Other Compensation
The firm does not compensate third parties for client referrals.

Item 15. Custody
The firm does not maintain custody of client assets; all assets are held at a qualified custodian.

Item 16. Investment Discretion
The firm exercises investment discretion pursuant to a written investment advisory agreement.

Item 17. Voting Client Securities
The firm does not vote client proxies.

Item 18. Financial Information
The firm has no financial condition that is reasonably likely to impair its ability to meet contractual commitments to clients.
